channel.title: Personal on 今日は, I am alebaffa !
channel.description: Recent content in Personal on 今日は, I am alebaffa !

item.title: Changing Everything. Again.
item.description: After 7 years spent in France I am officially moving to Tokyo, Japan, end of May 2017. I am finally going to join my wife, Sarah, after almost 5 years of long and hard distant relationship. Now, before to start with the burocracy madness and to move/sell/close/sign things, I am sitting here at home, in the first weekend after having resigned from my current company, trying to think about how many things I learned over the last 7 years.

item.title: The Paradox Of Choice In Learning to Code
item.description: I was talking with a guy some days ago and it went like this: he told me “I really would like to start improving myself and I need a pet project..“ I said “great idea! But where do you want to improve?“ he said “..that’s the problem: I still don’t know what I want to use. There are so many things out there that I would like to learn … and I don’t know where to start…“ This made me think about a book I read some time ago called “The Paradox of Choice: Why More Is Less” (the author gave also an awesome TED Talk about it).

item.title: Surrounding yourself with the right people
item.description: I was 8 years old when I started playing guitar. It was not my choice, though, but my parents’ who forced me to start taking some lessons. I hated it at the beginning: hours of solfège, and I wasn’t even able to play the most stupid song. No chords, nothing at all. Nevertheless I had some natural talent for music (I’m gifted with the so called musical ear. And so my brother and sister are - the Force is strong in my family), and maybe my parents knew that and that’s why they persisted so much with me.
